channel.title: Frontion - Global Geopolitical News
channel.description: Tier 1 geopolitical news headlines from Reuters, AP, BBC, AFP, Al Jazeera, and other authoritative sources.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇹🇼 Trump Plans Unprecedented Call With Taiwan President, Risking China Tensions
item.description: US President Donald Trump announced he would speak with Taiwan President Lai Ching-te, a move unprecedented for a US leader that could significantly strain US-China relations. The statement came days after Trump met with Chinese leader Xi Jinping.
item.author: Reuters

item.title: 🇨🇺🇺🇸 US Indicts Former Cuban President Raúl Castro Over 1996 Plane Shootdown
item.description: Federal prosecutors announced charges against former Cuban President Raúl Castro for his role in the 1996 downing of civilian planes operated by Miami-based exiles, escalating US pressure on Cuba. Trump said no escalation is expected.
item.author: Reuters

item.title: 🇷🇺🇨🇳 Putin and Xi Declare Ties at Unprecedented High in Beijing Summit
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin met Chinese leader Xi Jinping in Beijing, declaring bilateral ties at an unprecedented level just days after Xi hosted US President Trump. The summit underscored deepening Sino-Russian cooperation amid Western pressure.
item.author: NBC News

item.title: 🇺🇦⚔️ Zelenskyy Warns of Russian Offensive Threat From Belarus Front
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy warned of five attack scenarios Russia has drawn up for a potential northern offensive targeting Kyiv from Belarus, as Ukraine increases forces in the north.
item.author: The Guardian

item.title: 🇪🇺🛡️ NATO Chief Rutte Says US Troop Withdrawal From Europe Will Not Harm Defense
item.description: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte stated that planned US troop withdrawals from Europe will unfold gradually in a structured way and will not impact NATO defense plans, as Europe moves to strengthen its own defense capabilities.
item.author: Reuters

item.title: 🇪🇺 Rutte dismisses US military drawdown in Europe as expected
item.description: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte said reduced US military presence in Europe is normal as Trump administration shifts focus. European allies debate scaling up defense capabilities independently.
item.author: Politico

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan president warns foreign forces cannot decide island future
item.description: President Lai Ching-te stated that foreign powers cannot determine Taiwan future following Trump comments on arms sales to the island. China continues pressure campaign on the self-governing democracy.
item.author: France 24

item.title: 🛢️ Norway offshore oil output beats forecasts by 6.7%
item.description: Norwegian offshore production reached 2.158 million barrels per day in April, exceeding official forecasts by 6.7%. The surge highlights North Sea energy resilience amid global supply concerns.
item.author: Oil Price

item.title: 🇦🇪 UAE departure from OPEC reshapes global oil dynamics
item.description: UAE exit from OPEC raises questions about future oil pricing and supply coordination. The move could shift production strategy for one of the worlds largest oil exporters.
item.author: Economic Times

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran warns renewed US-Israel attack would expand conflict beyond region
item.description: IRGC says Tehran has not yet used all of its capabilities against adversaries. Warning comes amid fragile ceasefire and ongoing tensions in the Strait of Hormuz.
item.author: Anadolu Agency

item.title: 🇨🇳🇺🇸 China blocks Pentagon official visit over Taiwan arms package
item.description: Beijing refused to approve a visit by Pentagon Under-Secretary Elbridge Colby in protest of a $14 billion US weapons package for Taiwan, escalating US-China tensions.
item.author: Reuters

item.title: 🇬🇧💰 UK inflation cools as Iran war costs loom
item.description: British inflation fell more than expected to 2.8% in April, but the relief may be temporary as economic impacts from the Iran conflict are expected to hit households later this year.
item.author: Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇺 US charges Raúl Castro with murder over 1996 plane shootdown
item.description: The Justice Department unsealed an indictment charging former Cuban President Raúl Castro with murder for the 1996 downing of two civilian planes operated by Cuban exiles, amid escalating US pressure on Cuba.
item.author: Reuters

item.title: ⚔️🇷🇺 UK reports dangerous Russian jet intercept over Black Sea
item.description: Britain defense ministry said two Russian jets dangerously intercepted a RAF surveillance plane over the Black Sea last month, raising concerns about potential NATO-Russia escalation.
item.author: Reuters

item.title: 🇨🇩🦠 WHO warns Ebola outbreak growing in Congo
item.description: The World Health Organization reports 139 suspected deaths and 600 cases from a rare Bundibugyo Ebola strain in DRC, with no approved vaccine and outbreak expected to expand.
item.author: Reuters

item.title: 🇮🇷⚔️ Iran Displays Weapons at Public Events as Tehran Readies for Potential War Resumption
item.description: Iranian Revolutionary Guard members are conducting public weapons training in Tehran, with ballistic missiles displayed at mass weddings. The demonstrations aim to project strength and motivate hardliners amid economic hardship and war uncertainty.
item.author: AP News

item.title: 🚢 Iran Claims IRGC Coordinated Transit of 26 Vessels Through Hormuz in 24 Hours
item.description: Iran says its Revolutionary Guard is controlling traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, publishing a new map marking a controlled maritime zone. The FAO warned the blockade could trigger a severe global food price crisis within 6-12 months.
item.author: Al Jazeera

item.title: 🇦🇹🕵️ Austrian Ex-Intelligence Officer Found Guilty of Spying for Russia
item.description: Former Austrian intelligence official Egisto Ott was sentenced to over four years in prison for passing information to Russian intelligence and Wirecard fugitive Jan Marsalek. The case has revived concerns about Austria as a hub for Russian espionage.
item.author: BBC

item.title: 🇯🇵 Developed Asia draws private capital as investors pivot away from China
item.description: Japan is leading a shift in private capital allocation as LPs prioritize exits, stronger governance, and proven returns, with Australia and Korea also gaining attention from investors seeking alternatives to China exposure.
item.author: DealStreetAsia

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russia has finally turned its back on Europe, Berliner Zeitung reports
item.description: German newspaper Berliner Zeitung writes that Russia has reoriented itself to China, with EU-Russia trade shrinking from nearly $400 billion in 2013 to just $64 billion by 2025, an 80% decline since 2021.
item.author: EADaily

item.title: 🇹🇷 Erdogan tells Trump Iran issues can be resolved as Turkey mediates
item.description: Turkish President Erdogan told Trump he welcomed the ceasefire extension with Iran and believes contested issues can be resolved. Turkey has been passing messages between Washington, Tehran, and mediators Pakistan.
item.author: Cyprus Mail

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkey's parliament unites to demand US extradite FETO leader Gulen
item.description: Turkey's governing and opposition parties issued a joint declaration calling for the extradition of Fetullah Gulen, the US-based leader of FETO, which Turkey accuses of orchestrating the 2016 coup attempt that killed 251 people.
item.author: TRT World

item.title: 🇨🇺 US indicts former Cuban leader Raúl Castro over 1996 plane shootdown
item.description: The US Justice Department charged Castro with murder and conspiracy for ordering the downing of two civilian planes operated by Miami-based exile group Brothers to the Rescue, killing four people including three Americans. The indictment marks a sharp escalation in US pressure on Cuba's communist government.
item.author: BBC

item.title: ✈️ US military surveillance flights track movements near Cuba amid tensions
item.description: BBC Verify analysis shows US Navy P-8A Poseidon jets and MQ-4C Triton drones have been operating near Cuba since May 11, with some flights coming within 50 miles of the island. The public nature of the flights signals US intent to enforce the oil blockade and pressure Havana.
item.author: BBC

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russian jets dangerously intercept British spy plane over Black Sea
item.description: The UK Defence Ministry said two Russian Su-35 and Su-27 aircraft repeatedly intercepted an unarmed RAF Rivet Joint surveillance aircraft in international airspace in April, coming within six metres of the plane. Britain called it the most dangerous Russian action against a UK aircraft since 2022.
item.author: Al Jazeera
